chunk_summary: This section outlines estimated energy requirements for Indian children based on ICMR-NIN 2020 guidelines, providing age- and sex-specific calorie targets and practical strategies for clinical application.
learning_objectives: Understand age- and sex-specific energy requirements for Indian children as per ICMR-NIN 2020.
Learn to adjust energy targets based on activity level, growth needs, or weight concerns.
Apply practical strategies to translate calorie targets into meal planning and portion control.
Recognize common energy intake errors and methods to correct them in clinical practice.
key_facts: Energy requirements for Indian children (ICMR-NIN 2020): 0–6 months: 530 kcal/day; 6–12 months: 660 kcal/day; 1–3 years: 1110 kcal/day; 4–6 years: 1360 kcal/day; 7–9 years: 1700 kcal/day.
For boys: 10–12 years: 2220 kcal/day; 13–15 years: 2860 kcal/day; 16–18 years: 3320 kcal/day.
For girls: 10–12 years: 2060 kcal/day; 13–15 years: 2400 kcal/day; 16–18 years: 2500 kcal/day.
Energy needs per kg decrease with age, but younger children require more energy per kg than adolescents.
Adjust energy targets for high activity, cold exposure, or catch-up growth; reduce for excess weight gain or inactivity.
Practical strategies include correcting dilute feeds in infants, addressing skipped meals in adolescents, and limiting sugary beverages.
reference_quote: The ICMR-NIN 2020 brief note provides age- and sex-specific estimated energy requirements for moderate activity.
chunk_heading: MACRONUTRIENT NUTRITION IN CHILDHOOD;ESTIMATED ENERGY REQUIREMENTS
